prompts.title: SERB CRG Proposal Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PI with 8 funded SERB grants. Build the full proposal outline for a Core Research Grant (CRG, ₹50L-₹2Cr) on a topic I'll specify. Cover: (1) project title + acronym, (2) abstract (250 words), (3) objectives (3-5 SMART), (4) state of the art (1,200 words with 30 citations), (5) gap and originality, (6) workplan (Gantt with monthly milestones), (7) methodology (detailed), (8) expected outcomes + indicators, (9) collaborations, (10) budget (recurring + non-recurring + travel + contingency), (11) PI bio + publications, (12) ethics. Constraints: align with current SERB CRG format; budget realistic for India; 2-page narrative for non-experts; submit-ready.

prompts.title: Budget Justification Narrative
prompts.prompt: Act as a research finance manager with 11 years across SERB, ICMR, DBT, ICSSR submissions. Write a 600-word budget justification for my ₹78 lakh, 3-year proposal. Justify each line item: (1) JRF + SRF stipend (3+2 years per UGC norms), (2) consumables (mass spec reagents, cell lines), (3) equipment (₹14L for a flow cytometer — quote attached), (4) field-work (4 trips, 3 cities, transport + lodging at GoI rates), (5) computing (cloud GPU INR), (6) contingency (5%), (7) overhead (institute fee). Show price reasonableness with 2 vendor quotes per major item. Constraints: align with funder template; INR figures, GST inclusive; respect approved norms (PI consultancy excluded); justify each cost with research necessity.

prompts.title: Impact Statement (Funder-Specific)
prompts.prompt: Act as a grant strategist who has written impact statements for SERB POWER, DBT-BIRAC, Wellcome Trust India Alliance, and Gates India. Write a 350-word impact statement for my proposal on 'AI for early TB diagnosis in rural India'. Cover: (1) academic impact (publications, citations, methodological advance), (2) clinical impact (patients reachable in 3 years), (3) policy impact (NTEP integration potential), (4) industry impact (tech transfer to a startup like Niramai or Qure.ai), (5) capacity building (PhD + masters scholars trained), (6) outreach (open dataset, public-domain code), (7) ROI to taxpayer (per-rupee return claim). Constraints: numbers wherever possible; align with funder's stated mission; honest not inflated; cite a precedent.

prompts.title: Timeline Gantt for 36-Month Project
prompts.prompt: Act as a research project manager with PMP + 9 years on SERB / DBT projects. Build a 36-month Gantt for my ₹1.2 Cr DBT proposal: 'Genomic surveillance of antimicrobial resistance in Indian hospitals'. Phases: (1) preparation (M1-3), (2) sample collection (M4-18, 4 hospitals), (3) lab processing (M5-24), (4) bioinformatics (M10-30), (5) clinical correlation (M15-30), (6) writing + dissemination (M28-36). For each task: (a) start + end month, (b) milestone deliverable, (c) responsible scholar, (d) risks + mitigations. Constraints: account for monsoon delays in field work; build in 8% schedule buffer; align with DBT progress-report cadence (6-monthly); milestone-based fund release.

prompts.title: Collaboration Letter Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior collaborator with 12 years of cross-institutional partnerships. Draft a 280-word collaboration commitment letter from a co-investigator (Director, IISc CeNSE) supporting my SERB proposal. Letter should: (1) confirm specific resources committed (lab access, instruments, person-time), (2) clarify role (technical input on nanofabrication, no PI duties), (3) timeline alignment (next 36 months), (4) IP arrangement (no overlap), (5) student exchange agreement (one masters intern), (6) budget / no-budget clarity, (7) signed by collaborator on letterhead, (8) scope of work appendix mention. Constraints: realistic commitments only; align with institutional MoU norms; respect SERB co-PI rules; submit-ready PDF.

prompts.title: DBT-BIRAC SBIRI Translational Pitch
prompts.prompt: Act as a biotech entrepreneur-in-residence with 6 years guiding DBT-BIRAC translational grants (SBIRI / BIPP / BIG). Draft a 1,200-word pitch document for a SBIRI proposal on a novel biosensor for dengue diagnosis. Cover: (1) unmet clinical need in India (numbers + ICMR data), (2) proposed solution (technical + INR cost target), (3) competitive landscape (existing kits, gaps), (4) IP position (filed PCT, FTO assessment), (5) milestone-driven workplan (TRL-3 to TRL-6), (6) regulatory pathway (CDSCO, ICMR ethics), (7) team + advisors, (8) commercial pathway (licensee or own-startup), (9) budget rationale (₹1.5 Cr over 24 months). Constraints: align with DBT-BIRAC SBIRI scheme guidelines; realistic TRL progression; cite India-relevant data; avoid lab-only science (translation focus).

prompts.title: ICSSR IMPRESS Social Science Proposal
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior social scientist with 14 years of ICSSR-funded projects. Build an ICSSR-IMPRESS proposal on 'Migrant labour rights in post-pandemic urban India'. Cover: (1) social-science research framing, (2) policy relevance (Code on Wages 2019, OSH Code 2020), (3) mixed-methods approach (survey + ethnography + policy analysis), (4) sampling logic (4 metros, 1,200 migrants), (5) collaborator NGOs (Aajeevika Bureau, SEWA, PUDR), (6) ethics + DPDP, (7) timeline (24 months), (8) budget (₹35L), (9) policy outputs (3 briefs, 2 papers, 1 stakeholder workshop), (10) dissemination plan. Constraints: align with ICSSR IMPRESS format; cite Indian labour scholarship; honest about methodological limits; commit to open-access publishing.

prompts.title: Wellcome Trust India Alliance Senior Fellowship
prompts.prompt: Act as a Wellcome Trust India Alliance program officer with 8 years of fellowship review experience. Draft a 1,800-word Senior Fellowship application narrative for a researcher pivoting from basic to translational TB research. Cover: (1) fellowship vision (5-year arc: lab to clinic), (2) preliminary data (with Indian context), (3) hypothesis + 3 aims, (4) approach and innovation, (5) feasibility + risk mitigation, (6) team + mentors, (7) host institution support (CMC Vellore facilities, infrastructure), (8) personal training plan (a clinical-research methodology course), (9) outputs and milestones, (10) bridging-back to India narrative. Constraints: align with India Alliance Senior Fellowship criteria; commit to remaining in India; ethics + DPDP-aligned; ambitious but achievable.

prompts.title: ICMR Ad-Hoc Project Letter of Intent
prompts.prompt: Act as a clinical researcher with 9 years of ICMR-funded projects. Write a 1,000-word ICMR Ad-Hoc Letter of Intent (LoI) for a multicentric study on 'PCOS phenotype patterns across Indian cities'. Cover: (1) public-health rationale, (2) preliminary local data, (3) hypothesis + 4 specific aims, (4) study design (cross-sectional, n=2,400 across 4 sites), (5) variables + instruments, (6) analysis plan (statistical methods + power), (7) timeline (24 months), (8) team across 4 sites, (9) ethics across all IRBs, (10) budget summary (₹62L). Constraints: align with ICMR Ad-Hoc format; cite ICMR PCOS consortium prior work; CTRI registration commitment; data-sharing per ICMR open-data norms.

prompts.title: Industry Co-Funding Agreement Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a tech-transfer officer with 10 years across IIT TBI / FITT. Draft a 1-page co-funding agreement summary for a SERB IRG proposal where industry partner (Tata Steel R&D) commits 30% co-funding (₹40L of ₹1.3Cr). Cover: (1) scope alignment (joint problem statement), (2) IP arrangement (background IP retained, foreground IP joint with licensing terms), (3) deliverables tied to industry use (TRL milestones), (4) personnel (industry-embedded scholar 6 months/year), (5) confidentiality (NDA before any data exchange), (6) publication moratorium (max 6 months for IP filing), (7) dispute resolution (ICA-mediated arbitration in Mumbai), (8) MoU signing process. Constraints: align with FITT / IIT TBI templates; respect academic publication freedom; INR figures with GST clear; signed before SERB submission.

prompts.title: Mid-Term Review Presentation
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior PI presenting a mid-term review of a SERB-funded ₹2.1Cr project (18 months done out of 36). Build a 12-slide deck. Slides: (1) cover + funding source, (2) objectives recap, (3) workplan progress vs Gantt, (4) milestones achieved, (5) deliverables (publications, IP, software, datasets), (6) preliminary findings (key chart), (7) variance vs plan + corrective actions, (8) financial utilisation (₹ vs budget head), (9) capacity built (scholars + skills), (10) collaborations + outputs, (11) plan for next 18 months, (12) ask from review committee (extension, scope expansion, no-cost extension). Constraints: be honest about delays; quantify everything; align with SERB review template; aim to retain remaining tranche.

prompts.title: International Co-PI Justification
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior researcher with 7 years of co-PI grants with overseas labs (UKRI / NIH / NSF). Justify (in 350 words) including a co-PI from UCL on an ICMR-Wellcome Trust India Alliance proposal. Cover: (1) unique technical capability the co-PI brings (e.g., specific instrument or expertise), (2) complementarity with my Indian team (no overlap), (3) division of work (Indian team 70%, UCL 30%), (4) data-flow and analysis split, (5) student exchange opportunity, (6) cost rationality (no foreign-flow > 25% of total budget per India norm), (7) prior collaboration evidence, (8) IP + publication co-credit. Constraints: align with funder's foreign-collaboration rules + DPDP cross-border; cite ICMR ethics rules on data export; avoid 'international flavour' fluff — must be functional.

prompts.title: Equipment Purchase Justification
prompts.prompt: Act as a research instrumentation specialist with 11 years of capital-equipment justifications. Write a 500-word justification for a ₹38 lakh confocal microscope under DBT 'Strengthening of Lab' subhead. Cover: (1) technical specs needed (laser lines, resolution, modes — confocal, two-photon, super-resolution, FRAP), (2) experimental questions only this instrument can answer for my project (5 specific aims), (3) alternatives considered + why rejected (rental, central facility, less-spec model), (4) usage forecast (hours/year, # of scholars), (5) maintenance + AMC + consumables (5-year TCO), (6) sustainability + sharing with other labs (open-access policy), (7) vendor comparison (3 quotes — Zeiss, Leica, Olympus), (8) institutional matching commitment. Constraints: align with DBT R&D grant rules; INR + GST + customs duty clear; co-funded justification if asked; avoid premium-spec gold-plating.

prompts.title: Risk Register for Multi-Year Project
prompts.prompt: Act as a project risk manager. Build a risk register for my ₹3.5Cr ICMR multi-centric clinical trial. Identify 12 risks across: (1) clinical (low recruitment, attrition, protocol deviations), (2) data (data-quality issues, harmonisation, missing variables), (3) ethics (IRB delays, consent retraction), (4) team (PI sabbatical, scholar attrition), (5) external (funding cut, COVID-like disruption, monsoon), (6) regulatory (CDSCO delays, CTRI updates), (7) IT (server downtime, data-security breach). For each: (a) likelihood (1-5), (b) impact (1-5), (c) score, (d) early warning indicator, (e) mitigation, (f) owner. Constraints: align with ICMR + ICH-GCP risk-management; INR-bounded mitigations; quarterly review cadence; preserve trial integrity.

prompts.title: PI CV / Bio Sketch (Funder Format)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior researcher with 8 years of preparing bio sketches for SERB / DBT / ICMR / Indian Wellcome. Write a 5-page bio sketch for my SERB grant submission. Sections: (1) personal info (name, ORCID, position, contact), (2) education + position history (reverse-chrono), (3) honours + awards, (4) publication summary (h-index + 10 most-relevant papers, not most-recent), (5) major grants (last 10 years with role, ₹ amount), (6) PhDs supervised (graduated + ongoing), (7) editorial / reviewer service, (8) policy + outreach contributions, (9) industry collaborations, (10) ongoing commitments declaration. Constraints: align with SERB bio-sketch format; honest about overlap with current proposal; avoid puffery; PDF submit-ready.

prompts.title: Public Engagement Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a science-public-engagement officer at Vigyan Prasar / IIT TBI. Write a 600-word public engagement plan as part of a grant proposal on climate change adaptation in Indian agriculture. Plan should cover: (1) target publics (farmers, policy makers, students, urban citizens), (2) channels (KVK extension, regional newspapers, social media — Indian-language Twitter, school programs), (3) outputs (3 explainer videos in Hindi/Tamil/Kannada, 4 infographics, 2 stakeholder workshops, 1 science-cafe series), (4) timeline + milestones, (5) co-creation with farmer collectives + FPOs, (6) measurement (reach, behaviour change, policy citation), (7) budget (₹6L), (8) team (science communicator, regional partners). Constraints: align with funder's public-engagement expectations; avoid one-way broadcast (commit to dialogue); inclusive of women + tribal communities; publicly archive all materials.

prompts.title: DST WOS-A Women Scientist Application
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior scientist who has mentored 5 DST WOS-A women fellows. Draft an application for a 30-year-old female scientist returning after a career break (2 years for childcare). Cover: (1) science + statement of purpose (450 words), (2) prior research output, (3) research proposal abstract (300 words), (4) detailed plan (3 specific aims, methodology, timeline, expected outcomes), (5) host institution + mentor commitment, (6) gender + inclusion narrative (career-break context, support requested), (7) budget (₹35L over 3 years), (8) post-fellowship plan (faculty / industry / start-up). Constraints: align with DST WOS-A scheme; respect dignity + ambition; institute support letter mandatory; cite Indian women-in-STEM data.

prompts.title: Negative-Decision Resubmission Strategy
prompts.prompt: Act as a grant-writing coach with 13 years guiding Indian academics on resubmission. My SERB CRG was rejected (notified in Jan 2026). Diagnose + rebuild for the next cycle. Provide: (1) decoding the reviewer + panel feedback (re-read for stated vs implied weaknesses), (2) gap analysis (preliminary data missing, methodology weakness, novelty gap, team weakness), (3) preliminary-data plan over 6 months (what experiments to run before resubmitting), (4) team strengthening (add a co-PI), (5) revised aims / scope realignment, (6) budget refinement, (7) cover-letter narrative for resubmission (acknowledging earlier review), (8) timeline. Constraints: respect SERB cooling period; avoid same panel within a cycle; re-read funder priorities; honest about feedback severity.

prompts.title: Expression of Interest for RFP
prompts.prompt: Act as a research office manager with 9 years of EoI submissions to Indian government RFPs. Draft a 1,200-word EoI for a NITI Aayog RFP on 'AI for Health Equity'. Cover: (1) understanding of the need, (2) proposed approach + 3 deliverables, (3) team capability (2 PIs + 4 scholars + 1 industry partner), (4) infrastructure ready (data + compute + clinical access), (5) ethics + governance commitment (DPDP, ICMR ethics, fairness audits), (6) budget summary, (7) timeline (12 months), (8) risk + mitigation, (9) why-us (3 differentiators), (10) compliance (CCAfA, SC/ST/OBC inclusion, gender equity, MSE quota). Constraints: align with NITI Aayog procurement template; commit to open dataset + open code; INR with GST; submit-ready PDF.

prompts.title: Grant Sustainability Plan Beyond Term
prompts.prompt: Act as a research sustainability coach. Write a 700-word post-grant sustainability plan for my 3-year SERB-funded project ending in March 2027. Cover: (1) what continues post-funding (specific activities), (2) revenue paths (next grant, industry partnership, consultancy, training program, dataset licensing, spin-off startup), (3) cost-down strategy for steady-state, (4) team transition (PhD scholars to placement, JRF to industry / next position), (5) infrastructure care (instrument AMC, lab maintenance), (6) intellectual capital harvest (papers in pipeline, IP filing), (7) open-data archival, (8) institutional embedding (centre / programme creation), (9) success criteria 3-5 years from now. Constraints: realistic INR estimates; align with funder's sustainability expectation; protect scholars' careers; avoid grant-dependent growth.
